name: La Olla de San Vicente and the Puente Medieval on the Río Dobra — Cangas de Onís
name_original: La Olla de San Vicente y el Puente Medieval en el Río Dobra — Cangas de Onís
region_intro: Hiking around Amieva offers access to the Picos de Europa National Park, characterized by ancient mountains, deep valleys, and dramatic gorges. The region features impressive grey limestone massifs contrasted with lush green forests. Key natural features include the Río Dobra, the San Vicente Pool, and La Presa de la Jocica reservoir. This landscape provides diverse terrain for hiking. Best hiking trails around Amieva The most popular hiking route is La Olla de San Vicente and the Puente Medieval on the Río Dobra — Cangas de Onís, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) moderate trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete. This route features the San Vicente Pool and a medieval bridge over the Dobra River. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Collado del Angón to La Presa de la Jocica — Parque Nacional Picos de Europa, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.4 km) path. This route offers panoramic views from Collado del Angón and passes by the Jocica Reservoir. Local hikers also love the Amieva, Collado Angón, Senda del Arcediano — Parque Nacional Picos de Europa, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail leading through the historic Senda del Arcediano, often completed in about 3 hours 19 minutes. Hiking around Amieva is defined by limestone massifs, deep river valleys, and dense woodlands within the Picos de Europa National Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate river walks to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Amieva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 1,300 reviews. More than 9,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Amieva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Amieva?
A: Amieva offers a diverse range of hiking experiences with over 15 trails to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks within the Picos de Europa National Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Amieva?
A: Hiking in Amieva means encountering the dramatic landscapes of the Picos de Europa National Park. You'll find impressive grey limestone massifs, deep river valleys like the Río Dobra, and lush green forests. The terrain can vary from gentle paths along rivers to routes with significant elevation gain through mountainous areas.

Q: Are there hiking options suitable for families or beginners in Amieva?
A: Yes, Amieva has several trails that are well-suited for families and beginners. For instance, the route to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1184798929/la-olla-de-san-vicente-and-the-puente-medieval-on-the-rio-dobra-cangas-de-onis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Olla de San Vicente and the Puente Medieval on the Río Dobra&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate, shorter trail that allows you to enjoy natural pools and historical bridges without excessive difficulty. There are 4 easy routes and 8 moderate routes available, providing plenty of accessible choices.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails in Amieva?
A: Amieva's trails are rich with natural beauty and historical points of interest. You can discover the crystal-clear waters of the Río Dobra and the popular San Vicente Pool, ideal for a summer dip. Many routes also pass by La Presa de la Jocica (Jocica Reservoir). For historical context, parts of the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1220661311/amieva-collado-angon-senda-del-arcediano-parque-nacional-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Arcediano&lt;/a&gt;, a former Roman road, are also accessible.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views in Amieva?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its stunning vistas. The area around Collado del Angón provides panoramic views that showcase the vibrant green forests, the blue sky, and the grandeur of the surrounding Picos de Europa peaks. The Ordiales Scenic Balcony, though a more challenging ascent, offers spectacular views of the Angón valley, Amieva, Ponga, and a significant portion of Asturias.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Amieva?
A: Yes, Amieva offers several circular routes. A notable option is the loop from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208264071/el-collado-del-angon-to-la-presa-de-la-jocica-parque-nacional-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Collado del Angón to La Presa de la Jocica&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a rewarding circular experience with varied scenery. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picos de Europa – View of Jocica Reservoir loop from Collado de Angón&lt;/a&gt;, which is a more difficult, longer circular trail.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Amieva?
A: The best time to hike in Amieva is generally during the spring, summer, and early autumn months. During these periods, the weather is typically more stable, and the trails are more accessible. Summer offers the chance to swim in natural pools like San Vicente, while spring and autumn provide lush greenery and vibrant fall colors, respectively.

Q: Is it possible to hike in Amieva during winter?
A: Winter hiking in Amieva is possible but requires careful planning and appropriate gear due to potential snow and ice, especially at higher elevations within the Picos de Europa National Park. Some lower-altitude trails might remain accessible, but conditions can change rapidly, so it's essential to check local weather forecasts and trail conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for hiking in Amieva?
A: As Amieva is located within the Picos de Europa National Park, some specific activities or access to certain protected areas might require permits, especially for overnight stays or climbing. For general day hiking on marked trails, permits are typically not required, but it's always advisable to check with the National Park authority for the latest regulations before your visit.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Amieva?
A: The hiking experiences in Amieva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,300 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning natural beauty, the dramatic limestone landscapes contrasted with lush forests, and the variety of trails that cater to different fitness levels. The presence of features like the Río Dobra and the San Vicente Pool are also often highlighted as enjoyable aspects.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly hiking trails in Amieva?
A: Many trails in Amieva and the Picos de Europa National Park are generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's crucial to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas, to protect wildlife and livestock. Always check specific trail regulations or park guidelines before your hike.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Amieva?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many popular starting points for hikes in Amieva, especially those leading into the Picos de Europa National Park, will have designated parking areas. It's recommended to research specific trailheads in advance to confirm parking options and any potential fees, particularly during peak season.

Q: Are there any cafes or pubs in Amieva for after a hike?
A: Amieva and its surrounding villages offer a selection of local establishments where you can relax and refuel after a hike. You'll find traditional Asturian cafes and pubs that provide refreshments, local cuisine, and a welcoming atmosphere, perfect for unwinding and sharing your hiking stories.
tips: Well preserved and with great views of the river.
The Medieval Bridge of the Dobra is a stone bridge dating from the 13th century. It has been recognized as an Asset of Cultural Interest.
A spectacular setting. In summer you can bathe in the crystal clear waters.

waypoints.name: Suspension Bridge Over the Sella River
